title: fix: update EC2 hostname after instance restart
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Re-routing the hive
gist.blurb: Gave one of our core servers a quick reboot and updated the maps so the worker bees know exactly where to go. Everything is humming along smoothly!
gist.summary: Updated Cloudflare Worker bindings and internal scripts to route traffic to a newly assigned EC2 instance hostname.
gist.impact: Maintains seamless uptime for image and text generation APIs by ensuring all internal services point to the correct active server.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing a tiny hard hat, plugging a large ethernet cable into a new glowing server rack. The robot companion holding a clipboard with a new IP address. Cozy server room setting.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, plugging a thick cable into a glowing retro server rack. A chunky monitor-head robot stands nearby holding a clipboard. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
